node.Title: New HCP-Based Global Management Plane for Consul Now in Public Beta
node.Description: The new global management plane for Consul is now available as a free public beta. Try it out to gain full visibility for both self-managed and HCP Consul clusters.

node.Title: How to Connect to Kubernetes Clusters Using Boundary
node.Description: Here’s how to use HashiCorp Boundary to provide identity-based remote access and credential management for Kubernetes clusters.

node.Title: How to Become a Developer Advocate: Part 1, Public Speaking
node.Description: This is the first installment in a series of blog posts on how to build the skills needed to become a developer advocate (or similar technical roles). This post focuses on public speaking.

node.Title: HashiCorp Ambassador Call for Submissions 2023
node.Description: The submission window for HashiCorp Ambassador — our program to recognize individuals for knowledge sharing, mentorship, and kindness in the community — is now open.

node.Title: HashiCorp Awarded the Palo Alto Networks 2022 Global Technology Partner of the Year
node.Description: HashiCorp wins the 2022 Palo Alto Networks Global Technology Partner of the Year Award.

node.Title: HashiCorp 2022 Year in Review
node.Description: Before we ring in the new year, here’s a look back at some of the most important moments in 2022 for HashiCorp.

node.Title: HashiCorp Celebrates 10 Years
node.Description: Co-Founders Armon Dadgar and Mitchell Hashimoto share their thoughts on HashiCorp’s 10th Anniversary.

node.Title: New Terraform Run Tasks From Checkpoint, Rapid7, ZScaler and Others
node.Description: New Terraform Cloud Run Tasks partners provide more options to ensure security and compliance for cloud infrastructure management.

node.Title: HashiCorp Predicts: What to Expect in 2023
node.Description: Leaders from around HashiCorp share their predictions for the coming year on everything from Kubernetes to AI to zero trust security.

node.Title: Terraform Plugin Framework Is Now Generally Available
node.Description: The Terraform Plugin Framework 1.0 is now generally available to provide a modern and extensible way for developers to create Terraform providers.

node.Title: Dynamic Secrets for Waypoint with Vault
node.Description: Learn how to source dynamic secrets from HashiCorp Vault in your Waypoint deployments using dynamic configuration.

node.Title: HashiCorp Terraform and AWS: re:Invent Caps Off a Big Year
node.Description: AWS re:Invent 2022 is here. We highlighted what’s new with Terraform and AWS — like Launch Day support for new AWS services in the Terraform AWS Provider.

node.Title: re:Invent Roundup: HashiCorp Zero Trust Security and More with AWS
node.Description: Read our recap of HashiCorp security and networking news and developments on AWS from this past year.

node.Title: How HashiCorp Waypoint Works with Terraform, Vault, and Consul
node.Description: HashiCorp Waypoint uses plugins to accept configurations from Terraform, Consul, and Vault to simplify your application release orchestration workflow.

node.Title: HashiTalks 2023: 24-Hours of Virtual Knowledge Sharing
node.Description: HashiTalks returns on February 16, 2023. Join our global community for 24 hours of knowledge sharing. The call for speakers is open until December 7, 2022.

node.Title: AWS and HashiCorp's Journey to 1 Billion Terraform AWS Provider Downloads
node.Description: The HashiCorp Terraform AWS provider has surpassed one billion downloads — here’s how we got there, and what to look for next.

node.Title: Consul Service Mesh Support for AWS Lambda Now Generally Available
node.Description: HashiCorp Consul support for AWS Lambda is now generally available, enabling services in the mesh to invoke Lambda serverless functions.

node.Title: Consul 1.14 GA: Announcing Simplified Service Mesh Deployments
node.Description: HashiCorp Consul 1.14 introduces the Consul dataplane, service mesh traffic management across cluster peers, and service failover enhancements.

node.Title: Run Tasks Are Now Available in the Terraform Registry
node.Description: Run tasks can now be accessed from the HashiCorp Terraform Registry, helping developers discover run tasks they can integrate with their existing Terraform workflow.

node.Title: CDK for Terraform 0.14 Makes it Easier to Use Providers
node.Description: Cloud Development Kit for Terraform (CDKTF) 0.14 makes it easier to add and upgrade Terraform providers.

node.Title: HashiCorp at AWS re:Invent: Examining the State of Your Cloud Operating Model
node.Description: Accelerate your adoption of a cloud operating model. Visit us at AWS re:Invent in Las Vegas, Nov. 28 - Dec. 2 for breakout sessions, expert talks, and product demos.

node.Title: How Military Service Impacted Two HashiCorp Veterans
node.Description: Lisa Argueta and Joshua Blair from the Veterans ERG share how their time in the military shaped their workforce experience and how they find community at HashiCorp.

node.Title: Celebrating Mixed Identities with the Hashi:Blend ERG
node.Description: What does it mean to belong? Seven Hashi:Blend Employee Resource Group members talk about the impact multiracialism and multiculturalism have on their identity.

node.Title: How Telcos Can Add Mutual TLS to 5G Packet Core with HashiCorp Vault
node.Description: As next-generation 5G begins to take shape, learn about a suite of comprehensive, identity-based security solutions for microservice environments.

node.Title: The State of Vault and Kubernetes, and Future Plans
node.Description: Get an overview of the most common ways to use HashiCorp Vault and Kubernetes together, and get a preview of a new method we're considering.

node.Title: X.509 Certificate Management with Vault
node.Description: In this blog post, we’ll look at practical public key certificate management in HashiCorp Vault using dynamic secrets rotation.

node.Title: HashiConf Global 2022: From Zero Trust to No Code
node.Description: Check out the highlights from HashiConf Global 2022 and watch the 40+ keynote and session recordings now live on YouTube.

node.Description: Enhance your Terraform Cloud workflow by adding run tasks before the plan and/or apply stages.

node.Title: HashiCorp Wins Datadog’s Integration Developer Partner of the Year Award
node.Description: HashiCorp has won the 2022 Datadog Partner Network (DPN) Integration Developer Partner of the Year Award.

node.Title: New Competency Program Validates Systems Integrators’ HashiCorp Expertise
node.Description: The new Technical Competency Program for Systems Integrators allows partners to display earned competency badges on both their own website and HashiCorp.com.

node.Title: Cockroach Labs, Forgerock & PaloAlto Networks Highlight New Vault Integrations
node.Description: The HashiCorp Vault ecosystem saw multiple integrations from partners Cockroach Labs, Forgerock, and PaloAlto Networks as part of the 19 integrations completed this past quarter.

node.Title: How Two HashiCorp Employees Marked Latinx & Hispanic Heritage Month
node.Description: Karla De La Garza and Abraham Martinez, from HashiCorp’s Unidos Employee Resource Group, share their experiences in Mexico during Latinx & Hispanic Heritage Month.

node.Title: HashiCorp Vault 1.12 Adds New Secrets Engines, ADP Updates, and More
node.Description: The new HashiCorp Vault 1.12 focuses on improving core workflows and making key features production-ready.

node.Title: CDK for Terraform 0.13 Significantly Improves Performance
node.Description: Cloud Development Kit for Terraform (CDKTF) 0.13 vastly reduces synth time across all supported languages.

node.Title: Announcing HCP Waypoint Public Beta
node.Description: HashiCorp Waypoint — now available on the HashiCorp Cloud Platform in public beta — lets users standardize application delivery across platforms.

node.Title: Terraform Gains Visibility, Self-Service, and Compliance Upgrades
node.Description: Continuous validation, no-code provisioning, native OPA support for Terraform Cloud, and other new features are key upgrades to HashiCorp Terraform introduced at HashiConf Global 2022.

node.Title: HCP Boundary — Now GA — Bolsters HashiCorp's Zero Trust Security Solution
node.Description: General availability of HCP Boundary lets developers access critical systems with fine-grained authorizations — without managing credentials or exposing your network.

node.Title: HCP Vault on Microsoft Azure Now in Public Beta
node.Description: In addition to its availability on AWS, HCP Vault can now be deployed on Microsoft Azure infrastructure.

node.Title: HashiCorp Developer: Your New Experience for Docs and Tutorials
node.Description: HashiCorp Developer is your one-stop shop for interactive labs, sample code, videos, tutorials, and in-depth documentation.

node.Title: Consul 1.14 Beta: Announcing Simplified Service Mesh Deployments
node.Description: HashiCorp Consul 1.14 enhances traffic management and failover, and adds a new deployment method: Consul dataplane. AWS Lambda updates and beta Windows VM support were also added.

node.Title: Introducing a New HCP-Based Management Plane for Consul
node.Description: The HashiCorp Cloud Platform (HCP) adds new visibility and management controls for self-managed and HCP-managed Consul deployments.

node.Title: Announcing the 2022 HashiCorp Technology Partner Award Winners
node.Description: The 2022 HashiCorp Technology Partner Awards have been announced. Find out who the winners are in our four categories this year.

node.Title: Dungeons & Dragons for Team Building
node.Description: The HashiCorp Community team used a custom Dungeons & Dragons campaign as a team building exercise. Check out the highlights and then try it yourself.

node.Title: Terraform Enterprise Adds Run Metrics, Versioned Docs, and More
node.Description: Terraform Enterprise enhances observability and cuts operational burden with improved run metrics, identity-based storage authentication, and versioned documentation.

node.Title: Terraform 1.3 Improves Extensibility and Maintainability of Terraform Modules
node.Description: Now generally available, HashiCorp Terraform 1.3 introduces optional object type attributes with defaults, and enhancements to moved blocks, improving extensibility and maintainability of Terraform modules.

node.Title: Vagrant 2.3 Introduces Go Runtime
node.Description: HashiCorp Vagrant 2.3 introduces a new Vagrant executable written in Golang, tying the Vagrant architecture more closely into the broader HashiCorp ecosystem.

node.Title: How Drift Detection Helps Maintain a Secure Infrastructure
node.Description: Drift detection is essential to maintaining efficiency while minimizing risk and giving teams confidence in your infrastructure as code (IaC) provisioning workflow.

node.Title: Nomad 1.4 Beta Adds Nomad Variables and Updates Service Discovery
node.Description: The beta release of HashiCorp Nomad 1.4 enhances native service discovery support with health checks and introduces Nomad Variables, which lets users store configuration values.

node.Title: HashiCorp Tools Explained Using a Minecraft World
node.Description: Visit our Minecraft world and learn how HashiCorp Vault, Consul, Nomad, and Boundary all work through fun analogies. Join us at HashiConf Global 2022 — in Los Angeles or online Oct. 4-6.

node.Title: Waypoint 0.10 Brings Custom Pipelines and Nomad Plugin Updates
node.Description: HashiCorp Waypoint 0.10 — now generally available — introduces the tech preview of custom pipelines and improvements to the Nomad integration.

node.Title: F5, New Relic, and Sophos Highlight New Consul Integrations
node.Description: The HashiCorp Consul ecosystem continues its growth with the addition of 15 new self-managed, enterprise, and HCP Consul integrations.

node.Title: Consul-Terraform-Sync 0.7 Adds High Availability and Redundancy
node.Description: General availability of HashiCorp Consul-Terraform-Sync 0.7 represents a key step in the maturity of our Network Infrastructure Automation solution.

node.Title: Drift Detection for Terraform Cloud is Now Generally Available
node.Description: Drift Detection for Terraform Cloud continuously checks infrastructure state to detect and notify operators of any changes, minimizing risk, downtime, and costs.

node.Title: How To Make Interns a Dynamic Part of Your Team
node.Description: Figuring out where interns fit into larger team goals can be tough. One HashiCorp manager shares three essential tips to encourage success for everyone involved.

node.Title: Changes to the Terraform Partner Integration Program
node.Description: We are making some changes to the way that we tag modules and providers in the Terraform Registry for better consistency, clarity, and scalability in the Registry.

node.Title: HashiConf Global Preview: Evolving Your Cloud Operating Model
node.Description: Find out about the 13 most interesting HashiConf Global sessions for enterprise cloud platform teams. Join us October 4 - 6, 2022 in Los Angeles and virtual.

node.Description: The release of vault-k8s 1.0 marks a great time to learn how HashiCorp Vault has integrated with Kubernetes in the past and what to look forward to in the future.

node.Title: How to Standardize Cryptography in the Enterprise with HashiCorp Vault
node.Description: Learn how centralizing encryption tools in a self-service platform for developers lowers the cost of ownership, centralizes best practices, and makes auditing easier.

node.Title: What Can a Service Mesh Do?
node.Description: A service mesh is a force multiplier that can do everything from service discovery to zero trust security, load balancing, multi-cloud connectivity, automation, and north-south traffic.

node.Title: Refresh Secrets for Kubernetes Applications with Vault Agent
node.Description: Learn the system signal and live reload methods for updating Kubernetes applications when secrets change. See an example via a Spring Boot application.

node.Title: What HashiCorp Interns Learned This Summer

node.Title: Terraform Run Tasks Ecosystem Continues to Grow
node.Description: Teams can now integrate oak9 and Torq into the Terraform Cloud workflow via run tasks. Plus: A list of integrations now validated to work with Terraform Enterprise.

node.Title: How a Cloud Skills Shortage Is Affecting Multi-Cloud Adoption
node.Description: The 2022 State of Cloud Strategy survey reveals that IT organizations are turning to platform teams and automated tools to bridge a growing skills shortage.

node.Title: Nomad Service Discovery
node.Description: HashiCorp Nomad’s addition of native service discovery offers a new option for simple service registration, plus integration with Consul for more complex service mesh use cases.

node.Title: Consul API Gateway 0.4 Is Now Generally Available
node.Description: Consul API Gateway 0.4 introduces support for the new beta version of the Kubernetes Gateway API and HTTP path rewrites.

node.Description: Boundary 0.10 adds support for credential management of SSH keys and enhanced IAM workflows in the admin UI.

node.Title: Industrialized Workflows - Using Microsoft CAF Patterns and Terraform
node.Description: Learn how even non-technical operators can use HashiCorp Terraform Cloud to deploy Azure resources based on Microsoft's Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) templates.

node.Title: New Survey Shows Broad Cloud Waste — HashiCorp Terraform Can Help
node.Description: According to our 2022 State of Cloud Strategy Survey, 94% of organizations have avoidable cloud spending. See where the money’s going and how Terraform can help.

node.Title: Consul 1.13 Introduces Cluster Peering
node.Description: This release enhances a number of features in Consul while adding such new features as a Consul on Kubernetes CNI plugin and a beta version of cluster peering.

node.Title: Introducing Sentinel Policies to the Terraform Registry (Beta)
node.Description: Terraform Sentinel policies are now available in the Terraform Registry so you can publish policies you want to share and search the Registry for policies you need.

node.Title: Successful Multi-Cloud Adoption Requires Automated Security
node.Description: In HashiCorp’s new State of Cloud Strategy survey, 89% of respondents said that security is the key driver of their cloud success — here’s how to best secure your multi-cloud environment.

node.Title: Zero Trust Security for Kubernetes with a Service Mesh
node.Description: A service mesh like HashiCorp Consul can be an essential part of applying zero trust security principles to modern, complex, Kubernetes deployments.

node.Title: Cloud Platform Teams Are Everywhere — Here’s Why
node.Description: In HashiCorp’s new State of Cloud Strategy survey, 86% of respondents said they rely on cloud platform teams — for a wide variety of very good reasons.

node.Title: A Mother of Four Claims Her Seat at the Tech Table
node.Description: Zoraya Sanchez De La Vega Cayero makes a mid-career transition to tech via the HashiCorp Early Careers program.

node.Title: HashiCorp State of Cloud Strategy Survey 2022: Multi-Cloud Is Working
node.Description: Multi-cloud approaches — and platform teams — are helping 90% of respondents achieve their goals. But watch out for challenges around security, skills, and spending.

node.Title: 3 Surprising Things Consul Service Mesh Can Do
node.Description: HashiCorp Consul has evolved into a comprehensive networking platform that bolsters zero trust networking, works well with Kubernetes, and is remarkably easy to use.

node.Title: CDK for Terraform Is Now Generally Available
node.Description: Cloud Development Kit for Terraform (CDKTF) has reached its first GA release, adding full support for Go and providing a GitHub action to use with Terraform Cloud.

node.Title: Meet Some of HashiCorp’s Talented 2022 Interns
node.Description: Six interns in HashiCorp’s Early Career Program share their expectations, highs and lows, how they’ve built relationships, and advice for future interns.

node.Title: The Evolution of Service Networking
node.Description: In the journey toward a modern service-based networking solution, core workflows are needed for discovering services, securing service communications, automating networking tasks, and controlling access.

node.Title: HCP Consul on Azure is Now Generally Available
node.Description: HCP Consul on Microsoft Azure is now generally available and capable of supporting production workloads running on Azure. Get started today with a secure, resilient service mesh for connecting applications running in Azure.

node.Title: Cribl, MongoDB, and Thales Highlight New HCP and Enterprise Vault Integrations
node.Description: The HashiCorp Vault ecosystem continues to show strong growth with 12 new HCP, Enterprise, and OSS integrations added this quarter.

node.Title: Configuring Azure Application Gateway with Consul-Terraform-Sync
node.Description: Synchronize HCP Consul services as backend address pools for Microsoft Azure Application Gateway using Consul-Terraform-Sync (CTS).

node.Title: Recruiting for Early Careers at HashiCorp
node.Description: The second year of HashiCorp’s Early Career Program includes some 70 interns. The Early Career recruiting team shares details and offers tips for prospective candidates.

node.Title: Announcing Azure Stack Hub Provider 1.0
node.Description: The new HashiCorp Terraform Azure Stack provider 1.0 includes several provider behavior improvements, 19 new resources, and supports a new service for Key Vault.

node.Title: HashiCorp Wins Microsoft Azure OSS US Partner of the Year for 2022
node.Description: HashiCorp has been chosen as the 2022 Microsoft US (MSUS) Partner Award winner OSS on Azure and a 2022 Microsoft OSS on Azure Partner of the Year Award finalist.

node.Title: HashiConf Europe 2022 Recap: From Drift Detection to Our New Developer Site
node.Description: HashiConf Europe took place once again in Amsterdam this year. Watch our complete sessions playlist, read a recap of the big keynote announcements, and take a look back at the sights and sounds of our annual European community conference.

node.Title: Waypoint 0.9 Adds New Runner Commands
node.Description: HashiCorp Waypoint 0.9 introduces on-demand runner introspection, a runner install command, and support for HCP Waypoint.

node.Title: Reflections on Juneteenth
node.Description: Eight members of the HashiCorp Blacksmiths’ employee resource group share how they spent Juneteenth this year, and reflect on what they learned.

node.Title: Vault Logging and Alerting on Day 1
node.Description: Get a step-by-step guide to building a free solution for Day 1 Vault logging and alerting on AWS.

node.Title: Consul on Amazon ECS 0.5 Supports AWS IAM and Mesh Gateways
node.Description: The latest version of HashiCorp Consul on Amazon ECS adds support for AWS IAM authentication and mesh gateways.

node.Title: New on HCP: Boundary, Waypoint, Drift Detection, and Azure Support
node.Description: HashiCorp Cloud Platform has added several new capabilities, including managed services for HashiCorp Boundary and Waypoint, and Drift Detection for Terraform Cloud.

node.Title: HashiCorp Vault 1.11 Adds Kubernetes Secrets Engine, PKI Updates, and More
node.Description: Vault 1.11 focuses on improving Vault’s core workflows and making key features production-ready.

node.Title: Announcing Launch and Free Public Beta of HCP Boundary
node.Description: HashiCorp Boundary is now available as a managed service on the HashiCorp Cloud Platform in  free public beta to standardize secure remote access to critical infrastructure regardless of where it resides.

node.Title: Consul API Gateway 0.3 Introduces HA Logical Gateways
node.Description: The latest release of the HashiCorp Consul API Gateway allows users to generate multiple instances of a logical gateway — avoiding single points of failure.

node.Title: Consul 1.13 Tech Preview: Cluster Peering
node.Description: Cluster peering in Consul 1.13 offers a whole new model of how Consul handles cross-cluster federation.

node.Title: HCP Consul on Azure to go GA, Plus More Consul News
node.Description: HCP Consul is becoming generally available on Azure, Consul API Gateway version 0.3 is going GA, and Consul 1.13 is due later this year.

node.Title: New HashiCorp Developer Site is Now in Public Beta
node.Description: Our new documentation platform makes it easy to learn from dozens of interactive lab environments, hundreds of tutorials, and thousands of reference docs.

node.Title: Terraform Cloud Adds Drift Detection for Infrastructure Management
node.Description: Drift Detection for Terraform Cloud continuously checks infrastructure state to detect and notify operators of any changes, minimizing risk, downtime, and costs.

node.Title: Announcing the HCP Waypoint Private Beta Program
node.Description: The HCP Waypoint Private Beta Program allows select early adopters to onboard applications and try out HCP Waypoint to develop a proof of concept.

node.Title: The 3 Phases of Infrastructure Automation
node.Description: From adoption to standardization to operating and optimizing at scale, the evolution of infrastructure automation is critical to modern hybrid and multi-cloud environments.
